treatment_information.specific_treatment: lysine-free/low-tryptophan formula (XLYS, low TRY Analog, SHS International Ltd., UK)
treatment_information.dosage_or_details: Maintain lysine and tryptophan levels at the low ends of the normal ranges
treatment_information.treatment_response: Patients had normal intelligence, ranged between average to low average level and their brain magnetic resonance imaging showed only high intensity over deep white matter
Patients had normal intelligence, ranged between average to low average level and their brain magnetic resonance imaging showed only high intensity over deep white matter
Discontinued at 5 years of age. Currently, she exhibits no abnormal neurological signs

treatment_timeline.event: Seizure-like movement observed after vaccination, prescribed phenobarbital
No head control noted after a fever episode
Seizures suspected, prescribed baclofen and Lunimal (phenobarbital)
First stood with the assistance of gait orthosis
Patient 6 had focal seizures, treated with Diazepam

patient_outcome.final_status: All patients survived. Two patients had delayed development, but their brain lesions were resolved. The other four patients ran uneventful courses with normal intelligence.
patient_outcome.follow_up_details: Patients were followed up on every 3 months for amino acid analysis and acylcarnitine profiling. Brain magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) was also performed using standard methods.
patient_outcome.complications: One patient had encephalopathic crisis episodes prior to turning 1 year old that caused pallidal lesions. Another patient had a chronic progressive disease during infancy that caused bilateral putamen lesions.
patient_outcome.long_term_prognosis: Patients with GA-I diagnosed by newborn screening have promising outcomes, though the risks of disease progression prior to 1 year of age remain significant.
